subject: Human Resources
title: What the Gig Economy Is
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: What the Gig Economy Is.

hook.text: Thirty-six%of U.S. workers participate in gig economy—freelancers, contractors, and platform workers.

buildup.text: The gig economy consists of short-term, flexible, independent work arrangements—from Uber drivers to freelance consultants.

discovery.text: Tech platforms created the modern gig economy—matching workers to tasks instantly, without traditional employment relationships.

twist.text: Freedom for some, precarity for others. Many gig workers lack health insurance, retirement savings, and employment protections.

climax.text: HR must evolve to manage blended workforces—full-time, contractors, freelancers, and gig workers, all contributing to outcomes.

punchline.text: The gig economy gave workers freedom. It also gave them uncertainty.

quiz.question: What percentage of U.S. workers participate in the gig economy?
